faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: AVIF generally offers 10-20% better compression than WebP, but WebP has much broader browser support (97%+ vs ~93% for AVIF). For maximum compatibility, WebP is the safer choice.
faq.a2: Yes! WebP fully supports the alpha channel. Transparent areas in your AVIF images will be perfectly preserved in the WebP output.
faq.q3: Is it safe to convert images here?
faq.a3: Absolutely. Avifkit uses client-side processing technology. Your images are converted directly within your web browser memory and are never uploaded to any external server.
faq.a4: WebP is supported by Chrome 32+, Firefox 65+, Edge 18+, Safari 14+, and Opera 19+. This covers approximately 97% of global browser usage.
faq.q5: Can I use WebP on my website?
faq.a5: Yes! WebP is the recommended image format for modern websites. It's supported by all major browsers, CDNs, and CMS platforms. Use WebP to reduce page load times and improve Core Web Vitals.
faq.q6: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
faq.a6: Each file can be up to 10MB. Since processing happens entirely in your browser, larger files may take slightly longer depending on your device's performance.
faq.q7: Can I batch convert AVIF to WebP?
faq.a7: Yes! Upload up to 30 AVIF files at once. Click Convert All, then download individually or as a ZIP. All conversions run in parallel.
faq.q8: What quality setting should I use for WebP?
faq.a8: For most web images, 75-85% quality provides excellent results with small file sizes. Use 90%+ for photography where detail matters. Use 60-70% for thumbnails and decorative images.
faq.q9: Do I need to create an account?
faq.a9: No. Avifkit is completely free with no registration, no watermarks, and no usage limits. Just open the page and start converting.
